title: Floral Crumbly American Pear Cobbler
description: A warm, comforting American dessert featuring a mixture of sweet and tart flavors, crumbly topping, and a hint of floral notes, perfect for a cozy evening or special occasion.

ingredients.name: 1 cup pears, peeled and sliced (Bartlett or Anjou)
1/2 cup cold unsalted butter, cut into small pieces
1/2 cup granulated sugar-free sweetener (e.g., Swerve or Erythritol)
1/4 cup confectioners' sugar-free sweetener (e.g., Swerve or Erythritol)

instructions: In a large bowl, mix together sliced pears, currants, honey, lemon juice, and vanilla extract.
Roll out the chilled flour-butter mixture to form a 1/4-inch thick sheet, about 12 inches square.
Transfer the dough to a 9x9-inch baking dish and trim the edges to fit.
Pour the pear mixture into the baking dish and spread evenly.
In a small bowl, mix together granulated and confectioners' sugar-free sweetener, cinnamon, and a pinch of salt.
Crumble the sugar-free sweetener mixture evenly over the pear mixture.
Bake for 40-45 minutes or until the crust is golden brown and the fruit is tender.
Remove from oven and let cool for 10-15 minutes before serving. Drizzle with a hint of honey or sugar-free sweetener, if desired.
Deglaze the pan with a small amount of water or sugar-free sweetener to release any caramelized sugars and serve warm.
Serve warm with a scoop of vanilla ice cream or whipped cream, if desired.
